world_news.headline: News live: Trump renews accusations Australia hasn’t done enough to help in strait of Hormuz; PM to visit Geelong refinery after fire
world_news.summary:
Follow updates live

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

Marles says Trump has not made a ‘specific request’ relating to the strait of Hormuz

Marles, who is doing the media rounds this morning, has just been interviewed on ABC Radio National, where he said Trump has not made a “specific request” of Australia regarding the strait of Hormuz.

We’ve not received a specific request in relation to the strait of Hormuz, but we’ll work with all partners, our allies, and that very much includes the US in terms of whatever needs to be done in relation to the strait of Hormuz.

We’ve been working with the US.

We’ve been working with countries like the UK, with France.

When he criticises Australia, when he posts AI generated images of himself looking like Jesus. When he picks fights with the Pope. Do you believe that that undermines the trust that Australians have in the relationship?

Look, I’m not going to give a running commentary on the president’s social media posts.

Our relationship with the US deeply matters.

Continue reading...

world_news.headline: Middle East crisis live: Ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon takes effect; Hezbollah tells citizens to postpone returning home
world_news.summary:
Group urges caution as it says Israel has history of ‘breaking agreements’; Israeli prime minister says key demand is that Hezbollah must be dismantled

  • Trump announces 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon after ‘excellent conversations’

Iran has stopped all petrochemical exports to prioritise domestic supply and prevent shortages of raw materials, Reuters reported.

The state-owned National Petrochemical Company ordered firms to suspend exports until further notice.

world_news.headline: Netflix cofounder Hastings to step down after it lost Warner Bros deal
world_news.summary: The company's stock plunged about 8 percent on the news of Hastings's departure.

world_news.headline: US House votes to extend temporary protections for Haitians in Trump rebuke
world_news.summary: Ten Republicans join Democrats to extend Temporary Protected Status for nearly 350,000 Haitians in the United States.

world_news.headline: 'Shattered' Blue feels he let Carlton down in narrow loss to Collingwood
world_news.summary: Carlton coach Michael Voss says the club will support Elijah Hollands after his performance against Collingwood last night.

world_news.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree 10-day ceasefire, Trump says
world_news.summary: The US president invites the two countries' leaders to Washington as Israel's prime minister insists troops will not leave southern Lebanon.

world_news.headline: Turkiye grieves students killed in school shooting
world_news.summary: Hundreds turned out to the funerals of eight students who were fatally shot at school in Turkiye’s Kahramanmaras.

world_news.headline: Pope Leo urges peace in visit to Cameroon’s conflict-hit northwest
world_news.summary: Pope Leo XIV was in Bamenda in conflict-hit northwest Cameroon, urging peace in a region beset by deadly fighting.

world_news.headline: What is tailgating, and has FIFA banned it at World Cup stadiums in the US?
world_news.summary: Football fans in the US have criticised FIFA and local organisers over a move to ban tailgating at World Cup matches.

world_news.headline: A ceasefire in Lebanon — will it hold?
world_news.summary: After rare talks, a ceasefire in Lebanon raises one question: what happens on the ground next?
